paragraphs.body: Life moves fast. Certain days seem to go for forever because of classes, athletics, notifications and assignments to complete. The feeling of being overwhelmed by everything is most of the time surround my entire body. However, even on hectic days, we all need a space where we can feel the secure and the peace. This personal safe place is not just giving me comfort, but also helps me, most of the time to underline things that many might fail to notice.

A journal is a place to vent. Is a place that helps you to get rid of all your problems, even it is just for a period of time. Is a place where you can write your schedule, to feel more organized. For some, this works as a relaxation prosses when they have too many thinks to do at the same time. It can be a place where you can express any feelings that you have even it is one of hate or happiness, without people to judge you. It can be a home of your feelings that you feel insecure to share with other people. A journal is not necessary a place where you need to write correct every word, or your grammar to be perfect. Is not a person, that is why you will never be able to receive feedback. The journal will never have an opinion to your problems or will never get you a cart of goodies when you feel bad. Will never scream at you or punch you. At the same time, will never remind you what you wrote on it, will never give you notifications to remind you to write in it. Which makes the difference between your phone and a piece of paper. After all, a journal is like a mirror of our life. Is like a tool that help us to feel more connected to our roots. Is like a brush that help us to paint our story even if we are in the house that we born or in a park, on the other side of the planet.

annotations.comment: Let's talk about how we might leverage your definition paragraph to point more directly at your essay's central focusing idea.
annotations.context_left: feelings that you have even it is

paragraphs.body: As usual, my journal is in my bag,hidden among my other one hundred random things. But even I change my bags, my journal is all the time next to me, waiting to be scrawled with my thoughts. The cover is a beautiful navy blue that got my eye from day one. The gold spiral embraces all the pages creating in this way, day by day, story of my daily life. Most of the time, when I reach into my bag, my fingers brush against it, and it feels like a small moment of peace in the middle of all the chaos. Sometimes, I catch a subtle scent of ink from the pages, a reminder of when I accidentally spilled it, and that small aspect enhances the journal’s feeling of being truly mine. This item is more than just some thick pages. Is my safe place. Is a place where I can take a moment to pause and reflect, even if only for a minute. Doesn’t matter how hectic the day becomes, the jpurnal provides me with a peaceful momenta space to dream and allow my thoughts to wander freely. That is odd to it is that have so many colours in it and at the same time so many memories. If I look back sometimes is funny to read how I was thinking about some situations and how now, that I got through them, I can see them clearer. Because I got it exactly on the first day when I lead in America, I literally the new chapter of my life wrote on paper, which is a testament or proof of my unique journey and growth.

annotations.text: it feels like a small moment of peace in the middle of all the chaos. Sometimes, I catch a subtle scent of ink from the pages, a reminder of when I accidentally spilled it, and that small aspect enhances the journal’s feeling of being truly mine.

paragraphs.body: But the first day when I started to write my thoughts, was another moment of a new chapter. It was a day when the alarm was a loud one. The light made its way thought the big blinds, brightening the whole room. I knew it was Sunday, by how the smell of fresh coffee filled the air and surrounded me. My dad was calling me to come into the kitchen because the breakfast was ready. I swung my legs to the side of my bed, uncomfortable because of the coldness that is surrounding me. I stood up for some seconds, lifted my arms over my head, and stretched for a period of time. I went slow, with no rush, to the bathroom. There, the fresh water helped me to wake up properly. After my routine, I followed the smell the coffee and enter in the living room, where I found my parents, watching the news on TV. I was excited by the fact that my dad had made my favourite pancakes. The warmth of the mug in my hands made me think about the present moment and how good it is sometime to slow it down and to have a morning with no rush, no noise, just a peace of mind. Looking on the window, the birds were singing, flying between the young trees from my garden. Simple moments like this made me believe that life is more than just school and volleyball. Sometimes it is about to take it slow and to enjoy even the little things that, most of the time, I don’t even notice them because of how rushed I was.

annotations.text: light made its way thought the big blinds, brightening the whole room. I knew it was Sunday, by how the smell of fresh coffee filled the air and surrounded me.

The warmth of the mug in my hands made me think about the present moment and how good it is sometime to slow it down and to have a morning with no rush, no noise, just a peace of mind. Sometimes it is about to take it slow and to enjoy even the little things that, most of the time, I don’t even notice them because of how rushed I was.

paragraphs.body: And that was the moment that I was thinking that I need to write my thoughts on a piece of paper. It took place there, in my very first room, the one that I still remember the scent of fresh notebook, but at the same time the one of unfamiliarity. That day, when I decided to take a picture of the present moment and remember it for forever. It felt like descovering a piece of home I could take along with me. Every page provided me with a room to relax, to feel the smell of coffee that surrounded me and the sweet taste of my dad’s pancakes, like the first day.

annotations.text: Every page provided me with a room to relax, to feel the smell of coffee that surrounded me and the sweet taste of my dad’s pancakes,

paragraphs.body: The journal in that moment started to be a home to me. A home that I have it everywhere, even if I am in America or Romania. Through each chaotic doodle or thoughtful penned idea, is the feeling of safety, comfort. The feelings like when I was hearing the voices of my parents or their laughs. For me, this are one of the most beautiful sounds in the world. It makes me feel more me when I am with them.

annotations.text: A home that I have it everywhere, even if I am in America or Romania.
annotations.comment: Fascinating! This is where you reveal the metaphor of the object. Neat.
annotations.context_left: to be a home to me.

paragraphs.body: My journal, as you can see, had served as a silent friend during my crazy changes. He took a picture to all my thoughts when I found it hard to express them to the people around me. He provided a spot when my life felt overwhelming. He includes, from my best days in my life to the worst one, to schedules, to vents after practices to calm Sundays. I really can say that is a part of my identity, that is more than what my ID can say. It is more then, where I was born or when. It is my story. A story of a twenty years of living, in this world, full of crazy events. But at the time, i feel like all of us need to feel this feeling of being home, but not home. So, where do you go when you need to feel at home?
